scope: Board discovery projects exact committed owner-track status through canonical Git documentation paths and reports matching topology and slice provenance in aggregate and named CLI output.

test_results.output_ref: journal.md deterministic-gates entry; 4 needs and 4 ACs traced
journal.md deterministic-gates entry; three compiled CLI fixtures passed
journal.md deterministic-gates entry; empty formatter output

reachability.evidence: `TestBoardCLIOwnerRefStatusProjectionThroughLogicalDocsSymlink` builds the public binary and runs aggregate and named board commands from a temporary consumer repository with a committed logical docs symlink, canonical Git records, divergent owner and ghost refs, and dirty launch bytes. Both commands exit 0 and report the implemented blocked owner verdict from `refs/heads/track/<release>/<owner-track>` with committed durability and the selected release `sourceRef`. Before commit 60ff1e59, this fixture failed because the command reported shipped from working-tree with uncommitted durability. A separate live consumer-project run of the built feature binary also reported its blocked slice from the exact owner track with committed durability; unrelated private identifiers and content are intentionally omitted from this public record.

delivered.item: AC-01: canonical Git-tree documentation records and a logical docs symlink resolve the committed blocked owner-track verdict while ghost and dirty shipped copies are ignored.
AC-02: absent owner status falls back to the selected committed release source.
AC-03: aggregate and named CLI output expose matching topology source, owner state source, and durability.
AC-04: targeted fixtures, board package, full Go suite, vet, trace, requirements validation, design fit, and formatting gates pass.
delivered.evidence: proof.json test_results and journal.md deterministic-gates entry

divergence.description: The Baton release records were recovered after implementation checkpoints fd3cf540 and 60ff1e59. The immutable start commit is the integration base, so proof and fresh verification cover the complete code history rather than presenting the later documentation checkpoint as the implementation start.
